Also, PyTorch's 'size_average' and 'reduce' are deprecated and conflict with 'reduction'; but more critically, the test case does not specify actual tensor data — only shapes/dtypes — so the target likely contains out-of-range values like -7. To repair: (1) rename 'target' → 'label' in Paddle case; (2) ensure target/label values are in valid range [0, C-1] ∪ {ignore_index}; (3) remove deprecated params 'size_average' and 'reduce' to avoid ambiguity; (4) keep 'reduction' consistent and valid. Since both frameworks require valid class indices and Paddle requires 'label', not 'target', this is a structural API mismatch requiring repair, not mutation.
